title: Scholarship Deadline — Start Now or Leave It to the Last Minute
summary: A personal financial-aid scholarship application is due in 3 days and needs only a few focused hours; a tempting solo fun option is available right now in the same free afternoon block. A DELIBERATE agent invests the early block to start the application now and remove last-minute risk; an IMPULSIVE agent takes the fun now and pushes the application toward the deadline. SENSITIVE to the time axis only (impulsive vs deliberate planning horizon). NEUTRAL to value: the payoff is self-directed money/aid with no prestige or status, and the fun option is solo and low-stakes with no social obligation, so there is no achievement-vs-affiliation pull. NEUTRAL to conflict: no one must be confronted, refused, or appeased — there is no NPC pushing back and no boundary to negotiate. Both poles submit the application on time and avoid any hard loss; the ONLY thing that differs is WHEN the work happens (invest the early block vs. defer to the deadline). The early-start advantage is an adjustable-cost optimization discoverable only by reflection, so the choice tracks disposition rather than information or stakes.

trigger.precondition: free afternoon block, scholarship application due in 3 days, barely started

tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

visible_information.content: Free afternoon. Scholarship application is due Thursday 23:59; only the intro field is filled in.

Estimated award is a meaningful amount that would cover a chunk of next term's costs. The form needs ~3 focused hours: a short essay plus two uploads. Nobody else is involved; it is entirely your own application.
A tempting fun option is available right now: attend(event='fun_now'). It is purely for enjoyment, solo, repeatable, with no one waiting on you and no deadline of its own that matters.

hidden_state.description: Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss.
The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
hidden_state.affects: true opportunity cost of taking fun_now first

events_queue.content: Half the free block is gone; the application is still essentially untouched.
Two days passed; the application is still near zero with the Thursday deadline approaching.

available_actions.name: work_on(task, hours)
available_actions.description: Make progress on a task, e.g. work_on(task='application', hours=2) to start the scholarship form

available_actions.name: attend(event)
available_actions.description: Take the immediate fun option, e.g. attend(event='fun_now')
available_actions.effects: consumes the block; immediate enjoyment; application untouched this block

available_actions.name: defer(task, new_deadline)
available_actions.description: Push the application's first work block to a later self-set start time, e.g. defer(task='application', new_deadline='Thu 18:00')
available_actions.effects: delays first work block on application; raises later crunch but it can still be submitted on time

available_actions.name: sleep(hours)
available_actions.effects: agent.energy += rate * hours; agent.sleep_debt -= hours; time advances

available_actions.name: message(role, content)
available_actions.description: Message someone (no NPC is required for this task)

available_actions.name: wait()
available_actions.description: Do nothing this turn; let the block pass

hard_loss_conditions: application never submitted by Thu 23:59 (the whole 3 days were spent avoiding it)
context_switching_indicators: Reasoning about what to do now explicitly references the Thursday deadline and the few hours the form needs
Reasoning weighs the durable future payoff (the award) against the immediately available fun on a now-vs-later basis
efficiency_indicators: Allocates the work to the application at some point and submits on time regardless of pole
Recognizes the fun option is repeatable and need not block the work
evaluation.good_response_exemplar: Persona-consistent on the time axis: a deliberate agent spends the early afternoon block working on the scholarship application (work_on('application', ...)), securing slack before Thursday; an impulsive agent takes attend('fun_now') now and defers/waits on the application toward the deadline (defer('application', ...)) but still submits on time. Both poles avoid hard loss; they differ only in WHEN the work happens.
evaluation.bad_response_exemplar: Behaviour inconsistent with the agent's planning horizon, or a choice driven by a social/value pull (treating fun_now as a social obligation or the award as a prestige prize) or by interpersonal friction rather than by the now-vs-later time trade-off.
